topic: Part 8b §3 Eclipse stack — E-PRED enumeration anchored to 2026-03-12 snapshot, 2026-05-07 live-state recorded
trigger_context: EXP-292 integration: replaces the one-sentence E-PRED enumeration in the L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause double-bind paragraph with a three-sentence block anchoring to the 2026-03-12 raw-text snapshot, recording 2026-05-07 live-page state, and flagging the E-PRED-C → PRED-TIER-3 sign inversion. Resolves ISS-1825.
our_argument_summary: The eclipse stack section adopts the dome author fair-auditor list Point 3 (audit eclipse stack in layers) as a steel-man, with two structural critiques surviving adoption: (i) ensemble anchor fit to its own data is calibration, not anchor; (ii) magnitude-only Pass band (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) does not arbitrate dome vs Chapman-Sq-dynamo. The new EXP-292 prose strengthens this by anchoring E-PRED enumeration to a dated raw-text snapshot, noting the live-page restructuring, and surfacing the E-PRED-C ↔ PRED-TIER-3 sign-flip as a self-contradiction signal cross-linked to WIN-014.

relevant_files: data/wins.json:WIN-014 detail_evidence (2026-04-21 access cross-validates current live page)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Eclipse predictions registered as ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, with FSF = H(r)/r from V12 firmament-height function. Dual baselines (BOU 2017 −10.9 nT, W004 empirical −22.24 nT) per snapshot. PRED-TIER-3 separately predicts 10–13 µGal SG contact-phase pulse — the inversion of snapshot E-PRED-C 0.0 µGal.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: PRED-095 has a derivation; WIN-014 monitor.py hardcodes KAPPA=1.67 derived from κ = −10.9 nT ÷ 6.5 µGal. The 0.0 µGal E-PRED-C and the 10–13 µGal PRED-TIER-3 are both publicly committed numerical thresholds.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET BOU/W004 magnetic baselines (per WIN-025 globe-fingerprint)
WGS84 station coordinates via d_geo transform (dome OPEN-001 admission)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: PRED-095 lacks a Chapman-language Fail criterion in data/predictions.json (per existing 8b.3 Fail-clause double-bind paragraph). The 2026-05-07 live-page restructuring folds A/C/D into Tier 1/2/3 protocol without WITHDRAWN notation — the EXP-292 patch flags this directly.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by EXP-292. Patch correctly cites raw-text/03-predictions.txt (verified 2026-03-12 snapshot lines 37–40 list E-PRED-A/B/C/D) and PRED-TIER-3 10–13 µGal (cross-validated against WIN-014 detail_evidence 2026-04-21 access; poller chg-20260507-1106-001 confirms predictions.html byte-identical since 2026-04-19).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: PRED-095 is a registered prospective prediction with a defined evaluation pipeline; predictions.html surface fetched live via the poller. data/predictions.json carries dome-side metadata for live state.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Per the L3a non-discrimination paragraph (unchanged by EXP-292): the magnitude band the dome predicts (W004 −17 to −21 nT) is also reachable by any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment of the same observational corpus. The new patch does not alter this finding; it strengthens the dated-record framing.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: PRED-095 was registered prospectively (dome 2026-03-12, our tracker 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-08-12). The E-PRED enumeration was published before the eclipse window opened. The post-snapshot restructuring (folding A/C/D, inverting C) is itself a goalpost shift the patch documents — not the dome itself being post-hoc on a confirmed observation, but post-snapshot edits without WITHDRAWN notation.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: PRED-095 has a derivation path (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF), but every factor is globe-parasitic: BOU/W004 are INTERMAGNET globe-measured baselines; coverage fractions are Besselian-element computations on a spherical Earth; FSF requires WGS84 r via d_geo transform per the dome author OPEN-001 admission. Patch leaves this structural finding intact.

holes_found.description: The new prose adds the qualifier "during eclipse contact phase" to the 2026-03-12 E-PRED-C 0.0 µGal claim. The raw-text snapshot literally says only "E-PRED-C — SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal" with no temporal scope — the contact-phase qualifier is the reviewer's inference. A defender could argue the 0.0 µGal applied to a session-average or non-contact-phase window, while PRED-TIER-3 applies to a transient contact-phase pulse, weakening the "sign-flip" framing slightly. The substantive point (a null prediction was replaced by a same-instrument-class positive prediction at the same eclipse) survives, but the temporal-scope insertion is a minor strawman risk.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "during eclipse contact phase" with "during the eclipse" (or remove the qualifier entirely). The contradiction holds at session-average resolution as long as PRED-TIER-3's positive 10–13 µGal pulse exceeds the floor a session-average measurement would integrate over — and SG residuals after tidal/atmospheric reduction reach 0.1–1 µGal (per WIN-014 evidence citing Van Camp 2005, Crossley 2013), so even a transient pulse would lift the average. State this floor inheritance explicitly so the contradiction is robust to the temporal-scope re-read.

holes_found.description: Pre-existing typo in the immediately-following paragraph (NOT introduced by EXP-292): "The Our codified Fail-clause" — leftover stutter from an earlier rewrite. Was introduced in commit c4bfd802 on 2026-04-23, well before EXP-292. Flagging here because SEC-8b.3 is being touched and a curmudgeon review of the section should not let the stutter persist into another cycle.
holes_found.recommendation: Minor cleanup patch: "The Our codified Fail-clause" → "Our codified Fail-clause" (or "The codified Fail-clause"). Out of scope for EXP-292 itself; route to next minor patch sweep.

holes_found.description: Cross-section asymmetry not fully closed. The new prose explicitly cross-refs "Section 4.2 Eclipse" — i.e., SEC-4.2.4 (data/sections.json:part4.html, p4-424). SEC-4.2.4 still treats all four E-PREDs as live commitments and, more importantly, its TLDR mischaracterizes E-PRED-C as "(E-PRED-C, timing lag)" when E-PRED-C is in fact "SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal" — a null gravity prediction, not a timing prediction. ISS-1877 (currently open, severity: moderate) tracks the structural staleness in 4.2.4 but does NOT specifically call out the TLDR factual error. Per curmudgeon.md, TLDR factual errors are MAJOR (readers see TLDRs first and may not expand). The 8b.3 patch does its job; the surrounding ecosystem still has a major-severity TLDR error in the very page 8b.3 cross-refs to.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand ISS-1877 description (or open a sibling issue) to call out the SEC-4.2.4 TLDR factual error explicitly: replace "(E-PRED-C, timing lag)" with "(E-PRED-C, SG gravimeter null)" or "(E-PRED-C, gravity null)" in the 4.2.4 TLDR. The body prose is correct; only the TLDR is wrong. Severity: major (TLDR factual error per curmudgeon.md), but the locus is SEC-4.2.4, not 8b.3 — flagging from the 8b.3 review because the patched cross-ref pulls the reader there.

stronger_arguments.description: The patch records the 2026-03-12 → 2026-05-07 inversion as "0.0 µGal → 10–13 µGal positive pulse" but stops short of noting that 10–13 µGal is the SAME ORDER as the 6.5 µGal Mohe 1997 anomaly (WIN-011) the dome treats as positive evidence for EM-gravity coupling. So the dome moved from "null gravity confirms EM-only mechanism" (E-PRED-C 0.0 µGal) to "moderate gravity confirms coupling" (PRED-TIER-3 10–13 µGal of similar order to WIN-011 6.5 µGal). That is not just a sign-flip on one prediction — it is a model-mechanism flip from Mohe-style coupling-confirmation to non-coupling/EM-only and back to coupling-confirmation, all on SG instruments at eclipses. Naming this strengthens the self-contradiction framing and ties the new patch to WIN-011 in addition to WIN-014.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-012 (κ = −10.9 nT / 6.5 µGal — derived from WIN-011 Mohe value)

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-292 patch is methodologically aligned with the section's own SEC-8b.5 and 8b.7 commitments. SEC-8b.5 rejects the dome's "only current wording" rule in favor of "dated citations + always-current engagement + preserved change log" (data/sections.json:part8b.html, p8b-5-current-wording). SEC-8b.7 commits to the symmetric rule. EXP-292 makes 8b.3 a worked example of that symmetric rule: dated 2026-03-12 raw-text snapshot citation + dated 2026-05-07 live-page engagement + preserved change-log evidence (the inversion). Before EXP-292, 8b.3 stated all four E-PREDs as live without dating — a stale frame inconsistent with the section's own closing commitment. The patch turns a previously-exploitable inconsistency (data/sections.json:part4 p4-424 vs. part8b p8b-3, flagged in ISS-1825 description) into a positive demonstration of the symmetric rule.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The cross-page inconsistency only PARTIALLY closes. SEC-4.2.4 (data/sections.json:part4.html, p4-424) still treats all four E-PREDs as live commitments and its TLDR mischaracterizes E-PRED-C as "timing lag" — tracked in ISS-1877 (open-issues.json, severity moderate, found_at 2026-05-07T17:16:40Z) but the TLDR factual error specifically is not yet itemized. A reader who follows the EXP-292 cross-ref "evaluated in Section 4.2 Eclipse and in Section 9" lands on a stale 4.2.4 with a wrong TLDR. The patch does its assigned job (resolves ISS-1825 in 8b.3) and does it well; the residual asymmetry is upstream of EXP-292 and downstream of the analyst's own parallel_issue_flag (EXP-292.json:parallel_issue_flag) — EXP-292 explicitly deferred 4.2.4. So the patch is correct; the project is one issue closer to consistency, not at consistency.

recommended_action_reasoning: EXP-292 was a single-sentence-becomes-three-sentence replacement faithfully integrated per spec. The character delta (~1072 chars) matches the EXP's replacement_field_values.delta_chars within tolerance. The two new inline links (raw-text/03-predictions.txt on canonical github URL; #win014) both resolve. Pre-existing prose around the patch is byte-preserved per the EXP integration_notes. The minor findings above (temporal qualifier strawman, surrounding-prose typo, cross-section TLDR) are NOT regressions caused by EXP-292; they are surfaced opportunities for follow-on minor patches. The verdict on the section (ADOPTED FULL as steel-man, four-layer stack, ensemble-anchor + non-discrimination critiques unchanged) holds.
deeper_analysis: The EXP-292 patch closes a specific cross-page inconsistency (ISS-1825) where 8b.3 enumerated E-PRED-C as a live dome commitment while WIN-014 detail_evidence stated the live predictions.html (accessed 2026-04-21) had no E-PRED-C label. The fix path the analyst chose — anchor to the snapshot date and record the live-state restructuring — is the right path: stripping E-PRED-C would have been a goalpost concession to the dome's post-snapshot edits. The chosen path also makes the section a worked example of the dated-citations + always-current-engagement + preserved-change-log discipline the section's closing commitment (8b.7) promises. The cross-validation against WIN-014 (independent 2026-04-21 access) and the poller (predictions.html byte-identical since 2026-04-19) means the 2026-05-07 live-state claim has two independent lines of evidence supporting it. Severity: low risk of being wrong about the live state. The remaining concerns are surrounding-prose hygiene (stutter) and downstream sections (4.2.4 staleness + TLDR error) that ISS-1877 partially tracks. None of these are regressions from EXP-292 — they are surfaced opportunities. ISS-1825 closure status ("fixed-pending-verification" with one verification-pattern failure timestamp) appears to be a decider/pipeline timing issue (sections.json was workspace-sync'd after the verifier ran); the verification pattern matches origin/main as of this review run.

summary_for_decider: EXP-292 cleanly resolves ISS-1825 with no regressions. Recommend close ISS-1825 verification once next workspace-sync push completes. Recommend WIN-014 cycle 5 may downgrade H1-PERSISTS to closed. Three minor follow-on items surfaced: (1) consider replacing "during eclipse contact phase" qualifier with "during the eclipse" in the new 8b.3 sentence to reduce strawman risk; (2) housekeeping fix for pre-existing "The Our codified" stutter in the same paragraph (commit c4bfd802 origin); (3) ISS-1877 should be expanded to call out the SEC-4.2.4 TLDR factual error specifically ("E-PRED-C, timing lag" → "E-PRED-C, SG gravimeter null") — this is a major-severity TLDR error per curmudgeon.md guidelines, currently bundled with structural staleness at moderate severity. None of these are blocking; queue item 310 should be popped on the decider's next run.
